LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE ON THE ISSUE OF SUCCESSOR LIABILITY AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THIS ISSUE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO DECLARATIONS OF LUJAN AND AY ARE OVERRULED. THE DECLARATIONS MERELY PROVIDE A BASIS FOR EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS FROM FLEXITALLIC GASKETS. THEY ARE NOT RELEVANT TO THE SUCCESSOR LIABILITY ISSUE BUT WOULD BE RELEVANT IF THERE WAS A FACTUAL BASIS FOR SUCCESSOR LIABILITY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT INCORPORATES FACTS ON THE ISSUE OF SUCCESSOR LIABILITY. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJDICTION. Appearances: Laurel Halbany, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of Brydon Hugo & Parker (415) 808-0300, appearing for Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion Granted. Off Calendar Oct-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jane Weston of Vasquez Estrada & Dumont (453.0555) for defendant and moving party
Laurel Halbany of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence on the issue of successor liability and plaintiff failed to present evidence sufficient to raise a triable issue of fact on this issue. Defendant's objections to declarations of Lujan and Ay are overruled. The declarations merely provide a basis for exposure to asbestos from Flexitallic gaskets. They are not relevant to the successor liability issue but would be relevant if there was a factual basis for successor liability. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that incorporates facts on the issue of successor liability. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
